$GOOGL CEO Sundar Pichai reaffirmed the company’s $175B to $185B capex plan for 2026.

That keeps the AI infrastructure buildout firmly on track.
$ARM JUST HIT A NEW ALL-TIME HIGH
$ARM is a ~3% position in my family portfolio

Good to see $ASTS recovering through the week because BlueBird 7 being lost was not the same thing as AST’s technology failing because this was completely a Blue Origin issue. 

I also think the whole fiasco is a tailwind for $RKLB because it reminded the market how hard orbital execution really is and why reliability matters. Rocket Lab’s 21 Electron launches at 100% mission success in 2025 are exactly the kind of track record the market values more as the space economy matures.
NASDAQ HITS NEW ALL TIME HIGH

3 THINGS I’M LOOKING FOR IN $NOW EARNINGS

1. Real evidence the the AI pricing overhaul actually moves the needle or just sounds good on a call
2. Whether cRPO growth confirms the business is truly accelerating rather than just lapping easier comps
3. The 98% renewal rate & seat count hold up as AI-driven layoffs start pressuring enterprise headcount

I think ServiceNow has done a smart job repositioning the platform around managing humans, agents & machine identities inside one workflow stack but this quarter has to show that the new packaging is doing more than making the product easier to explain.
$AEHR JUST CROSSED $100  🤯

$SOFI is expanding its home lending platform with a fully digital HELOC offering and a new real estate agent network.

The move deepens SoFi’s push into end-to-end home lending across mortgages, refinancing, jumbo, FHA, VA and home equity products.

$AVGO expanded its $GOOGL Cloud partnership to support Cloud Network Insights which is a first-party service built on AppNeta technology.

The goal is to give customers better end-to-end visibility across multi-cloud and hybrid environments and speed up troubleshooting.
$GOOGL SAYS AI NOW GENERATES 75% OF ITS NEW CODE

$GOOGL just unveiled TPU 8t & TPU 8i showing how quickly AI infrastructure is splitting between training & inference:

• TPU 8t for frontier-model training
• TPU 8i for inference, agents & reasoning
• 124% more performance per watt for 8t
• 117% more performance per watt for 8i
• ~80% better performance per dollar for 8i

General availability is expected later this year.
$HOOD just announced that Robinhood Venture Fund has closed an investment in OpenAI worth ~$75M.

t3live.com/ppo

📺 MEMORY STOCKS NEED POWER & $TSLA INTO EARNINGS

Come to trade $TSLA earnings today with me & Alpha Team – secure.t3live.com/alpha-team…

Memory stocks like $MU and $SNDK are setting up, but they need real power and volume to break out, not just drift higher.

$MU looks closer to a move if it clears key levels, while $SNDK continues to face selling pressure near highs. 

The market remains highly selective, rewarding only true strength. 

Meanwhile, $TSLA into earnings is less about fundamentals and more about narrative, with a focus on future growth themes like autonomy and robotics. 

The approach here is simple: stay level-driven, avoid chasing, and commit only when momentum confirms.

I’m keeping it small with $TSLA options, not chasing the move.

 If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

📺 PULLBACK CANCELED? PRICE ACTION SAYS YES

The current market is mirroring last year’s April tariff period: a sharp rally off the lows, a clean break above resistance, followed by sideways consolidation—not a breakdown.

Last year, the market was ~21% off highs → rallied hard. Then pulled back ~9–10% → formed an inverted head & shoulders. Key leaders (Mag 7, semis, memory stocks) regained strength, which led to an explosive breakout to new highs.

So, this kind of pause is normal in a strong uptrend, not a sign of weakness.

*

If the market wasn’t strong, it would’ve failed at resistance. Instead of rejecting key levels, $SPX broke through resistance and continued higher toward ~7147.

This behavior signals that buyers are in control and the market is trend-confirming, not rolling over.

So, price action already answered the question.

*

Right now, the market is letting the 8-day moving average catch up. This usually happens when the price runs too fast, and the market needs to reset via time, not price.

So, we expected a pullback toward ~$698–$700 $SPY (prior highs/support zone).

Then came the news, which pushed the market higher instead of pulling back.

This is not weakness being avoided – it’s strength absorbing potential downside.

If bad news doesn’t push price down → that’s bullish. Period.

*

So, watch $SPY around ~$698–$700 (prior highs / support / 8-day region). 

Key questions – does the market hold this zone if tested? 

That determines continuation → bullish trend intact or breakdown → deeper pullback possible.

*

So, the market remains in a strong uptrend, now consolidating. No rejection at resistance = bullish signal. The current pause is healthy digestion, not weakness.

News didn’t break the market → strength confirmed. Now watch $SPY ~$698–$700 – it matters if tested.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
$SPX futures +38 as we see if early strength holds or fades, with 7147 as Friday’s high. There are plenty of opportunities, but pick your spots carefully and manage with a process.

Fasting taught me more about investing than any model.

Both require tolerating discomfort without reacting.
Both punish those who chase every signal.
Both reward a process — not a prediction.

The market, like hunger, passes.
Discipline, like compounding, accumulates.

Gold just printed a gravestone doji.

While everyone cheered the ceasefire extension, the hedge trade quietly cracked.

+36% in a year. -2.8% in a single session.

One chart tells you what's really unwinding.

leadlagreport.com/p/ceasefir…

Risk-on: equities up, credit tight, EM leads, dollar weak.
Risk-off: Treasuries rally, gold bid, yen strengthens, defensives lead.

Most investors watch one market. The edge is watching all of them simultaneously.

Divergence is the warning. $TLT $GLD $EEM

Learn more about the ATAC Credit Rotation ETF $JOJO at atacfunds.com/jojo/

Lead-Lag Publishing, LLC is not an affiliate of Tidal/Toroso or ACA/Foreside.
2007: Spreads were tight. Everyone was comfortable. Then Bear Stearns. Then Lehman. The lesson? Tight spreads are a setup, not a safety net. $JOJO knows the playbook.

A strong dollar is a wrecking ball for emerging markets.

EM nations borrow in USD. Dollar strength raises debt costs, triggers capital flight, and forces brutal rate choices.

The dollar cycle is a global liquidity mechanism.

Understand it first. $EEM $DXY

High-yield spreads have historically LED equity drawdowns — not followed them.

Credit markets see the cracks first.

When $HYG sells off while equities hold, watch carefully.

The bond market is rarely the last to know. $HYG $LQD

Small caps have underperformed large caps for an historically extended stretch.

Mean reversion is one of the most powerful forces in markets.

$IWM/$SPY relative strength at multi-decade lows.

Cycles turn. The question is what triggers the rotation. $IWM $SPY
I will not relent.

When $XLU outpaces $XLK, pay attention.

Defensive rotation into utilities has historically preceded volatility spikes by 3–6 weeks.

Smart money doesn't announce its exit. It rotates quietly.

Watch the lead, not the lag.
The S&P 500
